interactions.summary: Jiang rejects technology-only war, distinguishes Israel from Pax Judaica, models capital as parasite/host, predicts generational wealth transfer upward, explains America's fortress advantage, rejects math for game theory, and says public opinion does not matter in geopolitics.

speaker_notes.note: Focus refs are primarily Jiang speaking; embedded questions are Jiang reading or paraphrasing midterm/audience prompts unless separately noted.

claims.claim: America will lose the Iran war unless it can galvanize popular patriotism and political will; money and technology are not enough.
Pax Judaica is not identical with Israel; Israel is the host and Pax Judaica/transnational capital is the parasite.
Global war and chaos will transfer wealth from the young to the old because capital can foresee, distribute, and exploit risk.
America does not intentionally need to plan global-economy weakening; as a self-sufficient fortress it can survive consequences that damage the global economy, so it appears to win regardless.
Jiang says the two-week ceasefire will not hold because it is theater negotiated by failed intermediaries in a U.S. vassal state.
Jiang does not use math in game theory because random human behavior cannot be modeled with only a few hundred years of data; he uses intuition, doubt, debate, and imagination.
Public opinion does not matter in geopolitics; power matters, as shown by empires later worshiped despite violence.

signature_moments.moment: Spend $100 trillion if you want; without political will, America still loses.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I don't care how much money you put in this war
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It distills his anti-material theory of war.

signature_moments.moment: Pax Judaica is the parasite, Israel the host; later the United States can be the host too.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Israel as the host, Pax Judaica as the parasite
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is one of the episode's sharpest structural metaphors.

signature_moments.moment: Public opinion does not matter in geopolitics; what matters is power.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It answers the Israel legitimacy question with Jiang's stark geopolitical realism.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Model where transnational capital/Pax Judaica attaches to a state host such as America or Israel.
Self-sufficient America can retreat from global collapse into its own resource base.

chronology_notes.note: The midterm restates the Iran-war prediction as an American political-will failure, while distinguishing Israel from Pax Judaica.
